title: Add Friends Chat Bar with Friend List and 1000 Friend Limit

requirements.summary: Add a persistent chat bar UI element below the user profile section on FriendsModePage that opens a friends panel or drawer when clicked
acceptanceCriteria: A chat/friends bar is visible below the user profile section on FriendsModePage.tsx
Tapping or clicking the bar opens a friends panel or slide-up drawer
The bar is styled consistently with the Sonic visual theme (cobalt blue/yellow, Fredoka One or Nunito font, rounded styling)

file_operations.description: Add a persistent chat bar UI component below the existing profile section. The bar should display a 'Friends' label with a chat/users icon. On click, toggle a slide-up drawer or panel component that overlays the page content. Style the bar using Sonic theme colors (cobalt blue background, yellow accent, Fredoka One font) with rounded corners. Ensure the bar remains visible and accessible at all times on the page.

Create a drawer/panel component that slides up from the bottom (mobile) or appears as a modal overlay (desktop) when the chat bar is clicked. Include a close button, a header showing 'Friends', and a scrollable content area. Use Sonic-themed styling with rounded corners, blue/yellow color scheme, and shadcn/ui Sheet or Dialog primitives for accessibility. The component should accept isOpen and onClose props for controlled state.

requirements.summary: Display a 'No Friends' empty state message in the friends panel when the user has zero accepted friends
acceptanceCriteria: When the authenticated user has zero accepted friends, the panel shows a 'No Friends' empty state message
The empty state is styled consistently with the Sonic visual theme
No friend list items are rendered when the friend count is zero

file_operations.description: Add conditional rendering logic: when the friends list is empty (length === 0), display a centered empty state component with a friendly message such as 'No Friends Yet' and a subtitle encouraging the user to add friends. Use Sonic-themed icons (e.g., a Sonic character silhouette or ring icon) and maintain consistent typography (Nunito body text). Ensure no friend list items render when the count is zero.

requirements.summary: Display the friend list with a maximum of 1000 friends and show a 'Friend limit reached (1000/1000)' message when the cap is hit
acceptanceCriteria: The friends panel lists all accepted friends when the user has at least one friend
The total number of displayed friends cannot exceed 1000
When the friend count reaches 1000, the 'Add Friend' action is disabled and a 'Friend limit reached (1000/1000)' notice is shown
Friend relationships are persisted in the backend keyed by the user's principal
The backend enforces the 1000-friend cap and returns an error if exceeded

file_operations.description: Add a useGetFriends hook that calls the backend getFriends() method and returns an array of Principal objects representing accepted friends. Use React Query with appropriate caching and error handling. The hook should be enabled only when the user is authenticated. Include a isFetching state for loading UI.

Use the useGetFriends hook to fetch and display the friend list. Map over the friends array and render each friend as a list item showing their display name (fetch via getUserProfile for each Principal). Display a friend count badge in the panel header (e.g., 'Friends (5)'). When the friend count reaches 1000, display a warning banner at the top of the panel: 'Friend limit reached (1000/1000)' styled with a yellow background. Disable any 'Add Friend' UI elements when the count is 1000. Show loading skeletons while fetching friends.

Create a reusable component that renders a single friend item. Accept a Principal prop, fetch the user profile via useQuery and getUserProfile, and display the friend's name. Include a placeholder avatar (use a Sonic-themed icon or generated asset). Style the item with hover effects, rounded corners, and Sonic theme colors. Handle loading and error states gracefully.

requirements.summary: Display incoming pending friend requests in a 'Friend Requests' section with Accept and Decline buttons
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Friend Requests' section is shown in the friends panel when there are pending incoming requests
Each pending request shows the requester's display name
Accept and Decline buttons are present for each request
Accepting a request moves the user into the friends list without a full page reload
Declining a request removes it from the pending list

file_operations.description: Add useGetFriendRequests and useRespondToFriendRequest hooks. useGetFriendRequests calls backend getFriendRequests() and returns an array of FriendRequest objects. useRespondToFriendRequest is a mutation hook that calls backend respondToFriendRequest(requesterId: Principal, accept: boolean), invalidates the friend requests and friends queries on success, and shows a toast notification. Include proper error handling and optimistic updates.

Add a 'Friend Requests' section above the friends list in the panel. Use the useGetFriendRequests hook to fetch pending requests. Conditionally render this section only when there are pending requests (length > 0). Display a section header 'Friend Requests' with a count badge. Map over the requests and render each using a FriendRequestItem component. Ensure the section is visually distinct from the friends list (e.g., lighter background or border separator).

Create a component that renders a single friend request. Accept a FriendRequest prop, fetch the requester's profile via getUserProfile to display their name. Include two action buttons: 'Accept' (green, calls respondToFriendRequest with accept=true) and 'Decline' (red/gray, calls respondToFriendRequest with accept=false). Use the useRespondToFriendRequest mutation hook. Disable buttons while the mutation is pending. Style consistently with the Sonic theme using rounded buttons and appropriate spacing.
